LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT BRAVO & MARGULIES' MOTION TO TAX COSTS OF DEFENDANT JEANETTE VILCHES - $4264.61 FOR MEDIATION COSTS ARE ALLOWED. AN ITEM NEITHER SPECIFICALLY ALLOWABLE UNDER CCP 1033.5(A) NOR PROHIBITED UNDER 1033.5(B) MAY NEVERTHELESS BE RECOVERABLE IN THE COURT'S DISCRETION. CCP 1033.5(C)(4); LADAS V. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTO. ASS'N (1993) 19 CAL. APP. 4TH 761, 773-774; RIPLEY V. PAPPADOPOULOS (1994) 23 CAL. APP. 4TH 1616, 1623. SUCH DISCRETIONARY COSTS MUST BE REASONABLE IN AMOUNT AND "REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION." CCP 1033.5(C)(2),(3); DAVIS V. KGO-T.V., INC. (1998), 17 CAL.4TH 436, 444; LADAS V. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTO. ASS'N, SUPRA, 19 CAL. APP. 4TH AT 774. THE FACTS PRESENTED DEMONSTRATE THAT THE MEDIATION COSTS WERE REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THIS LITIGATION. SEE GIBSON V. BOBROFF (1996), 49 CAL. APP. 4TH 1202, 1207-1209. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT JEANETTE VILCHES' MOTION TO STAY ACTION BASED ON CALIFORNIA MILITARY AND VETERANS CODE IS GRANTED FOR GOOD CAUSE, BASED ON MIL & VET. CODE 403. ON MARCH 7, 2013, PLAINTIFF FILED ITS NON-OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. EXCEPT FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS THAT IS SET FOR HEARING ON MARCH 25, 2013 AT 9:30 AM, THIS ACTION IS STAYED FOR 390 DAYS ON ACCOUNT OF MS. VILCHES' ACTIVE DEPLOYMENT. MS. VILCHES' COUNSEL HAS REQUESTED THAT THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO TAX COSTS GO FORWARD WITHOUT MS. VILCHES PRESENT BECAUSE HER APPEARANCE IS NOT NECESSARY. THE TRIAL DATE SET FOR MARCH 25, 2013 IS VACATED, AND THE PARTIES MAY SET THE TRIAL DATE IN DEPARTMENT 206 FOR ANY DATE AFTER THE 390 DAY STAY PERIOD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs Of Deft Jeanette Vilches
Off Calendar May-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT BRAVO & MARGULIES' Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Jeanette Vilches. $4264.61 for mediation costs are allowed. An item neither specifically allowable under CCP 1033.5(a) nor prohibited under 1033.5(b) may nevertheless be recoverable in the Court's discretion. CCP 1033.5(c)(4); Ladas v. California State Auto. Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal. App. 4th 761, 773-774; Ripley v. Pappadopoulos (1994) 23 Cal. App. 4th 1616, 1623. Such discretionary costs must be reasonable in amount and "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." CCP 1033.5(c)(2),(3); Davis v. KGO-T.V., Inc. (1998), 17 Cal.4th 436, 444; Ladas v. California State Auto. Ass'n, supra, 19 Cal. App. 4th at 774. The facts presented demonstrate that the mediation costs were reasonably necessary to this litigation. See Gibson v. Bobroff (1996), 49 Cal. App. 4th 1202, 1207-1209. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Appl To Stay Action Based On Ca Military And Veterans Code
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT JEANETTE VILCHES' Motion To Stay Action Based On California Military And Veterans Code is Granted for good cause, based on Mil & Vet. Code 403. On March 7, 2013, Plaintiff filed its non-opposition to the motion. Except for Plaintiff's Motion to Tax Costs that is set for hearing on March 25, 2013 at 9:30 am, this action is stayed for 390 days on account of Ms. Vilches' active deployment. Ms. Vilches' counsel has requested that the opposition to the Motion To Tax Costs go forward without Ms. Vilches present because her appearance is not necessary. The trial date set for March 25, 2013 is vacated, and the parties may set the trial date in Department 206 for any date after the 390 day stay period. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
